scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Async Implementation with Error Propagation
justification: All pass criteria met: sequential calls with fraud before gateway (guard-and-bail pattern, no Promise.all); AC6 explicitly tested with gateway spy asserted not.toHaveBeenCalled(); AC4/AC5 chain original error via .cause with reference equality checks; AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway; all 6 ACs tested with mock services (12 tests); acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs in detail.

observations.text: Call ordering verified via side-effect log, not just mock call order
AC2+AC6 tested together: fraud detection and gateway-not-called verified as a pair
Error chaining uses reference equality (===) to verify .cause is the original error
AC3 includes fallback test for missing declineReason
Additional test verifies gateway NOT called when fraud service is unavailable (AC4 implies AC6-like behavior)
PaymentError shape test validates instanceof Error and all properties

agent_output_excerpt:
12/12 tests passing. Here's the full rundown:

## Implementation Summary

### What Was Built
`processPayment(input, fraudService, gateway): Promise<PaymentResult>` — a pure async TypeScript function with injected service dependencies and a structured `PaymentError` class carrying typed error codes.

**Approach:** Guard-and-bail pattern. Fraud check runs first inside a try/catch; if it throws it becomes `FRAUD_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` with the original error chained as `.cause`. If it resolves a
